sections.paragraph:
ἀναπνέω 1 aor. ἀνέπνευσα (s. πνέω; Hom. et al.; also Job 9:18; Philo, Joseph.) draw breath, revive ἀνέπνευσεν ἡ ματρῶνα the (married) woman (Artemilla) revived (from a swoon) AcPl Ha 4, 2.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 70
